let's fix this 1) **Severity: High**  
**Title:** Idempotency cache is globally keyed and replay can cross endpoint/user/tenant boundaries  
**Conclusion:** **Fail**  
**Evidence:** `repo/backend/core/middleware.py:139`, `repo/backend/core/middleware.py:140`, `repo/backend/core/models.py:83`  
**Impact:** A reused `Idempotency-Key` can return a cached response body not scoped to request identity/path, risking data leakage and incorrect action replay semantics.  
**Minimum actionable fix:** Scope idempotency records by `(key, endpoint, actor_id/tenant_id)` and validate all dimensions on lookup before replay.

2) **Severity: High**  
**Title:** Webhook secrets are returned in normal API responses  
**Conclusion:** **Fail**  
**Evidence:** `repo/backend/integrations/serializers.py:72`, `repo/backend/integrations/views.py:237`, `repo/backend/integrations/views.py:294`  
**Impact:** Secret disclosure increases blast radius for internal endpoint compromise and violates least-privilege handling of credentials.  
**Minimum actionable fix:** Mark `secret` write-only; return masked value (or omit entirely) on list/detail/read operations.

76	class IdempotencyRecord(models.Model):
77	    """
78	    Stores results of non-idempotent API calls so repeated requests
79	    with the same key return the cached response without re-processing.
80	    Records older than 24 hours may be hard-deleted by a scheduled task.
81	    """
82	    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
83	    key = models.CharField(max_length=255, unique=True, db_index=True)
84	    endpoint = models.CharField(max_length=500)
85	    response_status = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField()
86	    response_body = models.JSONField()
87	    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True, db_index=True)
88	
89	    class Meta:
90	        db_table = "core_idempotency_record"
91

229	    """
230	    GET  /api/v1/integrations/webhooks/  — list all endpoints for tenant
231	    POST /api/v1/integrations/webhooks/  — create a new endpoint
232	    """
233	    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated]
234	
235	    def get(self, request):
236	        _require_admin(request)
237	        endpoints = WebhookEndpoint.objects.filter(tenant=request.user.tenant)
238	        return Response(WebhookEndpointSerializer(endpoints, many=True).data)
239	
240	    @transaction.atomic

242	        _require_admin(request)
243	
244	        url = request.data.get("url", "").strip()
245	        _validate_webhook_url(url)
246	
247	        ser = WebhookEndpointSerializer(data=request.data)
248	        ser.is_valid(raise_exception=True)
249	
250	        endpoint = WebhookEndpoint.objects.create(
251	            tenant    = request.user.tenant,
252	            url       = ser.validated_data["url"],
253	            secret    = ser.validated_data["secret"],
254	            is_active = ser.validated_data.get("is_active", True),
255	            events    = ser.validated_data.get("events", []),
256	        )
257	        _log(request, AuditLog.Action.CREATE, "WebhookEndpoint", endpoint.pk,
258	             {"url": endpoint.url})
259	        return Response(WebhookEndpointSerializer(endpoint).data, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)

108	class IdempotencyMiddleware:
109	    """
110	    Caches POST responses by the Idempotency-Key request header.
111	
112	    On first request with a given key → processes normally, caches the
113	    JSON response body and status code in IdempotencyRecord.
114	
115	    On repeat request with the same key (within 24 h) → returns the cached
116	    response immediately without re-executing the view.
117	
118	    Only 2xx JSON responses are cached. Non-JSON and error responses are
119	    passed through unchanged.
120	    """
121	
122	    def __init__(self, get_response):
123	        self.get_response = get_response

130	        return result.quantize(Decimal("0.01"), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)

132	    @transaction.atomic
133	    def activate(self):
134	        """
135	        Transition self (DRAFT) → ACTIVE.
136	
137	        If a currently ACTIVE version exists:
138	          1. self.effective_from must be strictly after that version's effective_from.
139	          2. Supersede that version: status → SUPERSEDED, effective_to = self.effective_from - 1 day.
140	        Then set self → ACTIVE.
141	        """
142	        if self.status != self.Status.DRAFT:
143	            raise UnprocessableEntity(
144	                f"Only DRAFT versions can be activated (current status: {self.status})."
145	            )
146	
147	        # Lock the recipe row to prevent concurrent activations
148	        recipe = Recipe.objects.select_for_update().get(pk=self.recipe_id)
149	
150	        current_active = (
151	            RecipeVersion.objects
152	            .select_for_update()
153	            .filter(recipe=recipe, status=self.Status.ACTIVE)
154	            .first()
155	        )
156	
157	        if current_active:
158	            if self.effective_from <= current_active.effective_from:
159	                raise UnprocessableEntity(
160	                    "New version's effective_from must be strictly after the current "
161	                    f"active version's effective_from ({current_active.effective_from})."
162	                )
163	            # Supersede the current active version
164	            current_active.status       = self.Status.SUPERSEDED
165	            current_active.effective_to = self.effective_from - timedelta(days=1)
166	            current_active.save(update_fields=["status", "effective_to"])
167	
168	        self.status = self.Status.ACTIVE
169	        self.save(update_fields=["status"])
170

172	        """
173	        Transition self (ACTIVE) → ARCHIVED. ADMIN-only — enforced in view.
174	        """
175	        if self.status != self.Status.ACTIVE:
176	            raise UnprocessableEntity(
177	                f"Only ACTIVE versions can be archived (current status: {self.status})."
178	            )
179	        self.status = self.Status.ARCHIVED
180	        self.save(update_fields=["status"])

166	            current_active.save(update_fields=["status", "effective_to"])
167	
168	        self.status = self.Status.ACTIVE
169	        self.save(update_fields=["status"])
170	
171	    def archive(self):
172	        """
173	        Transition self (ACTIVE) → ARCHIVED. ADMIN-only — enforced in view.
174	        """
175	        if self.status != self.Status.ACTIVE:
176	            raise UnprocessableEntity(
177	                f"Only ACTIVE versions can be archived (current status: {self.status})."
178	            )
179	        self.status = self.Status.ARCHIVED
180	        self.save(update_fields=["status"])
181

188	
189	    class Unit(models.TextChoices):
190	        OZ    = "oz",    "Ounce"
191	        LB    = "lb",    "Pound"
192	        CUP   = "cup",   "Cup"
193	        TBSP  = "tbsp",  "Tablespoon"
194	        TSP   = "tsp",   "Teaspoon"
195	        FL_OZ = "fl_oz", "Fluid Ounce"
196	        GAL   = "gal",   "Gallon"
197	        QT    = "qt",    "Quart"
198	        PT    = "pt",    "Pint"
199	        EACH  = "each",  "Each"
200	        PINCH = "pinch", "Pinch"
201

207	    )

213	    )

215	    unit_cost       = models.DecimalField(

217	        decimal_places=4,
218	        validators=[MinValueValidator(Decimal("0"))],
219	    )
220	    sort_order      = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
221	
222	    class Meta:
223	        db_table = "foodservice_recipe_ingredient"
224	        ordering = ["sort_order", "id"]
225	
226	    def __str__(self):
227	        return f"{self.ingredient_name} ({self.quantity} {self.unit})"

1	"""
2	tenants/models.py
3	
4	Multi-tenant isolation layer.  Every operational table carries a tenant_id FK
5	so data never leaks across organizational boundaries.
6	"""
7	import uuid
8	from django.db import models
9	
10	
11	class Tenant(models.Model):
12	    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
13	    name = models.CharField(max_length=255, unique=True)
14	    slug = models.SlugField(max_length=100, unique=True)
15	    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
16	    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
17	    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
18	
19	    class Meta:
20	        db_table = "tenants_tenant"
21	
22	    def __str__(self):
23	        return self.name
24	
25	
26	class Site(models.Model):
27	    """A physical location (campus, building) that belongs to a Tenant."""
28	
29	    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
30	    tenant = models.ForeignKey(
31	        Tenant, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name="sites"
32	    )
33	    name = models.CharField(max_length=255)
34	    address = models.TextField(blank=True, default="")
35	    # IANA timezone string, e.g. "America/New_York"
36	    timezone = models.CharField(max_length=64, default="America/New_York")
37	    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
38	    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
39	    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
40	
41	    class Meta:
42	        db_table = "tenants_site"
43	        constraints = [
44	            models.UniqueConstraint(
45	                fields=["tenant", "name"], name="uq_site_tenant_name"
46	            )
47	        ]
48	
49	    def __str__(self):
50	        return f"{self.name} ({self.tenant.slug})"

For issue 3, I need to think about whether to add a site FK directly to `RecipeVersion` or create a separate model. Adding the FK directly is simpler but would be a breaking change since existing versions don't have a site. I should consider a migration strategy that handles this gracefully.

The audit report is clear: recipes need to be site-aware to enforce the "one active version per site" rule. The cleanest approach is adding a nullable `site` FK to `RecipeVersion` where null represents tenant-wide recipes, then updating the unique constraint to `(recipe, site)`. This keeps the existing model mostly intact while enabling the site-scoped behavior we need.

However, I'm realizing MySQL's NULL handling in unique constraints is problematic—NULL values don't compare as equal, so the constraint won't actually prevent multiple null entries. For MySQL specifically, I'd need a different approach, possibly using a join table or a workaround at the service layer to enforce the constraint when site is null. better approach is to use a sentinel value like an empty UUID or a special "all sites" concept.

Actually, looking at this more carefully - the minimum actionable fix is to add site scoping. Let me think about the approach:

Option A: Add `site` (nullable FK) to `RecipeVersion`, update unique constraint to `(recipe, site)` - works for PostgreSQL but not for MySQL NULL behavior.

Option B: Create a separate `RecipeSiteRelease` model that captures which version is active for which site.

Option C: Add `site` FK to `RecipeVersion` (non-null, requiring a site for each version) - breaking change.

Given MySQL is being used (based on docker-compose.yml), and the minimum actionable fix requirement, I think Option A with a nullable site field is the way to go. The `activate()` method already does a `select_for_update()` and checks programmatically, so I can enforce the `(recipe, site)` uniqueness at the application layer rather than relying on database constraints. For MySQL, partial unique constraints with conditions aren't supported the same way as PostgreSQL, so the existing `UniqueConstraint` with a `status = 'ACTIVE'` condition might not work as expected.

The fix is to add `site` to the unique constraint fields and update `activate()` to scope the check to the `(recipe, site)` pair. This gives the right semantics in PostgreSQL and provides application-level enforcement for all databases.

61	class RecipeVersion(models.Model):
62	
63	    class Status(models.TextChoices):
64	        DRAFT      = "DRAFT",      "Draft"
65	        ACTIVE     = "ACTIVE",     "Active"
66	        SUPERSEDED = "SUPERSEDED", "Superseded"
67	        ARCHIVED   = "ARCHIVED",   "Archived"
68	
69	    id             = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
70	    recipe         = models.ForeignKey(
71	        Recipe,
72	        on_delete=models.CASCADE,
73	        related_name="versions",
74	    )
75	    version_number = models.PositiveIntegerField()
76	    effective_from = models.DateField()
77	    effective_to   = models.DateField(null=True, blank=True)
78	    status         = models.CharField(
79	        max_length=20,
80	        choices=Status.choices,
81	        default=Status.DRAFT,
82	    )
83	    servings       = models.DecimalField(
84	        max_digits=10,
85	        decimal_places=4,
86	        default=Decimal("1"),
87	        validators=[MinValueValidator(Decimal("0.0001"))],
88	    )
89	    created_by     = models.ForeignKey(
90	        "iam.User",
91	        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
92	        related_name="created_recipe_versions",
93	    )
94	    created_at     = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
95	
96	    class Meta:
97	        db_table = "foodservice_recipe_version"
98	        ordering = ["-version_number"]
99	        constraints = [
100	            models.UniqueConstraint(
101	                fields=["recipe"],
102	                condition=models.Q(status="ACTIVE"),
103	                name="uq_recipe_one_active_version",
104	            ),
105	            models.UniqueConstraint(
106	                fields=["recipe", "version_number"],
107	                name="uq_recipe_version_number",
108	            ),
109	        ]
110	
111	    def __str__(self):
112	        return f"{self.recipe.name} v{self.version_number} [{self.status}]"
113	
114	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
115	    # Business logic
116	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
117	
118	    def compute_per_serving_cost(self) -> Decimal:
119	        """
120	        SUM(quantity * unit_cost) / servings, rounded HALF_UP to 2 dp.
121	        Returns Decimal("0.00") if no ingredients.
122	        """
123	        total = sum(
124	            ing.quantity * ing.unit_cost
125	            for ing in self.ingredients.all()
126	        )
127	        if not total:
128	            return Decimal("0.00")
129	        result = Decimal(str(total)) / Decimal(str(self.servings))
130	        return result.quantize(Decimal("0.01"), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)
131	
132	    @transaction.atomic
133	    def activate(self):
134	        """
135	        Transition self (DRAFT) → ACTIVE.
136	
137	        If a currently ACTIVE version exists:
138	          1. self.effective_from must be strictly after that version's effective_from.
139	          2. Supersede that version: status → SUPERSEDED, effective_to = self.effective_from - 1 day.
140	        Then set self → ACTIVE.
141	        """
142	        if self.status != self.Status.DRAFT:
143	            raise UnprocessableEntity(
144	                f"Only DRAFT versions can be activated (current status: {self.status})."
145	            )
146	
147	        # Lock the recipe row to prevent concurrent activations
148	        recipe = Recipe.objects.select_for_update().get(pk=self.recipe_id)
149	
150	        current_active = (
151	            RecipeVersion.objects
152	            .select_for_update()
153	            .filter(recipe=recipe, status=self.Status.ACTIVE)
154	            .first()
155	        )
156	
157	        if current_active:
158	            if self.effective_from <= current_active.effective_from:
159	                raise UnprocessableEntity(
160	                    "New version's effective_from must be strictly after the current "
161	                    f"active version's effective_from ({current_active.effective_from})."
162	                )
163	            # Supersede the current active version
164	            current_active.status       = self.Status.SUPERSEDED
165	            current_active.effective_to = self.effective_from - timedelta(days=1)
166	            current_active.save(update_fields=["status", "effective_to"])
167	
168	        self.status = self.Status.ACTIVE
169	        self.save(update_fields=["status"])
170	
171	    def archive(self):
172	        """
173	        Transition self (ACTIVE) → ARCHIVED. ADMIN-only — enforced in view.
174	        """

    def activate(self):
        """
        Transition self (DRAFT) → ACTIVE.

        If a currently ACTIVE version exists:
          1. self.effective_from must be strictly after that version's effective_from.
          2. Supersede that version: status → SUPERSEDED, effective_to = self.effective_from - 1 day.
        Then set self → ACTIVE.
        """
        if self.status != self.Status.DRAFT:
            raise UnprocessableEntity(
                f"Only DRAFT versions can be activated (current status: {self.status})."
            )

        # Lock the recipe row to prevent concurrent activations
        recipe = Recipe.objects.select_for_update().get(pk=self.recipe_id)

        current_active = (
            RecipeVersion.objects
            .select_for_update()
            .filter(recipe=recipe, status=self.Status.ACTIVE)
            .first()
        )", "new_string": "    @transaction.atomic
    def activate(self):
        """
        Transition self (DRAFT) → ACTIVE, scoped to (recipe, site).

        If a currently ACTIVE version exists for the same (recipe, site):
          1. self.effective_from must be strictly after that version's effective_from.
          2. Supersede that version: status → SUPERSEDED, effective_to = self.effective_from - 1 day.
        Then set self → ACTIVE.
        """
        if self.status != self.Status.DRAFT:
            raise UnprocessableEntity(
                f"Only DRAFT versions can be activated (current status: {self.status})."
            )

        # Lock the recipe row to prevent concurrent activations for this recipe+site
        recipe = Recipe.objects.select_for_update().get(pk=self.recipe_id)

        current_active = (
            RecipeVersion.objects
            .select_for_update()
            .filter(recipe=recipe, site=self.site, status=self.Status.ACTIVE)
            .first()
        )"}

             {"version_number": version.version_number})", "new_string": "    def post(self, request, pk, vid):
        recipe  = get_object_or_404(Recipe, pk=pk, tenant=request.user.tenant)
        version = get_object_or_404(RecipeVersion, pk=vid, recipe=recipe)

        # Optional site scoping: caller may POST {"site_id": "<uuid>"} to activate
        # for a specific site only; omitting it means tenant-wide (site=None).
        site_id = request.data.get("site_id")
        if site_id:
            site = get_object_or_404(Site, pk=site_id, tenant=request.user.tenant)
            version.site = site
            version.save(update_fields=["site"])

        version.activate()          # raises UnprocessableEntity on invalid state
        _log(request, AuditLog.Action.PUBLISH, recipe,
             {"version_number": version.version_number, "site_id": str(site_id) if site_id else None})"}

        recipe  = get_object_or_404(Recipe, pk=pk, tenant=request.user.tenant)
        version = get_object_or_404(RecipeVersion, pk=vid, recipe=recipe)
        version.activate()          # raises UnprocessableEntity on invalid state
        _log(request, AuditLog.Action.PUBLISH, recipe,
             {"version_number": version.version_number})", "new_string": "    def post(self, request, pk, vid):
        recipe  = get_object_or_404(Recipe, pk=pk, tenant=request.user.tenant)
        version = get_object_or_404(RecipeVersion, pk=vid, recipe=recipe)

        # Optional site scoping: caller may POST {"site_id": "<uuid>"} to activate
        # for a specific site only; omitting it means tenant-wide (site=None).
        site_id = request.data.get("site_id")
        if site_id:
            site = get_object_or_404(Site, pk=site_id, tenant=request.user.tenant)
            version.site = site
            version.save(update_fields=["site"])

        version.activate()          # raises UnprocessableEntity on invalid state
        _log(request, AuditLog.Action.PUBLISH, recipe,
             {"version_number": version.version_number, "site_id": str(site_id) if site_id else None})"}

_raw_enc_key = os.environ.get("FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY", "")
if not _raw_enc_key:
    raise _ImproperlyConfigured(

try:

        raise ValueError(f"Decoded key is {len(_decoded)} bytes; expected 32.")

    ) from _exc
FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY = _raw_enc_key

class Migration(migrations.Migration):
    """
    Scopes IdempotencyRecord by (key, endpoint, actor_id) so cached responses
    can never cross user or path boundaries.

    NOTE: partial DDL from prior failed runs already applied to this DB:
      - the standalone index on `key` has been dropped
      - the `actor_id` column has been added
    Use SeparateDatabaseAndState to sync Django's state without re-running
    those operations, then apply the remaining composite unique constraint.
    """

    dependencies = [
        ("core", "0003_request_log"),
    ]

    operations = [
        # Sync Django state: key no longer has a standalone index
        migrations.SeparateDatabaseAndState(
            database_operations=[],
            state_operations=[
                migrations.AlterField(
                    model_name="idempotencyrecord",
                    name="key",
                    field=models.CharField(max_length=255),
                ),
            ],
        ),
        # Sync Django state: actor_id column already exists in the DB
        migrations.SeparateDatabaseAndState(
            database_operations=[],
            state_operations=[
                migrations.AddField(
                    model_name="idempotencyrecord",
                    name="actor_id",
                    field=models.CharField(blank=True, default="anonymous", max_length=100),
                ),
            ],
        ),
        # Apply the composite unique constraint (not yet in DB)
        migrations.AlterUniqueTogether(
            name="idempotencyrecord",
            unique_together={("key", "endpoint", "actor_id")},
        ),
    ]
"}

Now write a clean migration that works from scratch — `AlterField` drops the unique index, `AddField` adds `actor_id`, and `RunSQL` with prefix lengths handles the composite unique (MySQL key-length limit), with `SeparateDatabaseAndState` only for the index step:

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/Users/muhammed/projects/eaglepoint/w2t61/repo/backend/core/migrations/0004_idempotency_scope_by_endpoint_actor.py", "content": "from django.db import migrations, models


class Migration(migrations.Migration):
    """
    Scopes IdempotencyRecord by (key, endpoint, actor_id) so cached responses
    can never cross user or path boundaries.

    MySQL key-length note: (key(255) + endpoint(500) + actor_id(100)) × 4 bytes
    (utf8mb4) = 3420 bytes > InnoDB 3072-byte limit.  The UNIQUE INDEX is
    therefore created via RunSQL with prefix lengths that fit within the limit
    while still enforcing uniqueness for all realistic values.
    """

    dependencies = [
        ("core", "0003_request_log"),
    ]

    operations = [
        # Remove the global unique constraint on key alone
        migrations.AlterField(
            model_name="idempotencyrecord",
            name="key",
            field=models.CharField(max_length=255),
        ),
        # Add actor_id to scope the record to a specific user
        migrations.AddField(
            model_name="idempotencyrecord",
            name="actor_id",
            field=models.CharField(blank=True, default="anonymous", max_length=100),
        ),
        # Composite unique index scoped by (key, endpoint, actor_id).
        # DB: prefix index to satisfy MySQL's 3072-byte InnoDB key-length limit.
        # State: AlterUniqueTogether so Django's ORM tracks the constraint.
        migrations.SeparateDatabaseAndState(
            database_operations=[
                migrations.RunSQL(
                    sql=(
                        "CREATE UNIQUE INDEX core_idempotency_key_ep_actor_uq "
                        "ON core_idempotency_record (`key`(191), endpoint(191), actor_id(100));"
                    ),
                    reverse_sql=(
                        "DROP INDEX core_idempotency_key_ep_actor_uq "
                        "ON core_idempotency_record;"
                    ),
                ),
            ],
            state_operations=[
                migrations.AlterUniqueTogether(
                    name="idempotencyrecord",
                    unique_together={("key", "endpoint", "actor_id")},
                ),
            ],
        ),
    ]
"}

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# run_tests.sh — Run all backend and frontend tests.
3	# Usage:
4	#   ./run_tests.sh             # run both suites
5	#   ./run_tests.sh --backend   # backend only
6	#   ./run_tests.sh --frontend  # frontend only
7	#   ./run_tests.sh --coverage  # backend with coverage report
8	
9	set -euo pipefail
10	
11	DC="docker compose"
12	BACKEND_FAILED=0
13	FRONTEND_FAILED=0
14	
15	RED='\033[0;31m'
16	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
17	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
18	BLUE='\033[1;34m'
19	NC='\033[0m'
20	
21	RUN_BACKEND=true
22	RUN_FRONTEND=true
23	COVERAGE=false
24	
25	for arg in "$@"; do
26	  case $arg in
27	    --backend)  RUN_FRONTEND=false ;;
28	    --frontend) RUN_BACKEND=false ;;
29	    --coverage) COVERAGE=true ;;
30	  esac
31	done
32	
33	echo ""
34	echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
35	echo -e "${BLUE}          HarborOps Test Runner           ${NC}"
36	echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
37	echo ""
38	
39	# ── Backend ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
40	if [ "$RUN_BACKEND" = true ]; then
41	  echo -e "${BLUE}▶  Backend (pytest)${NC}"
42	  echo "──────────────────────────────────────────"
43	
44	  if [ "$COVERAGE" = true ]; then
45	    PYTEST_CMD="pytest tests/ -v \
46	      --cov=core --cov=iam --cov=tenants --cov=assets \
47	      --cov=foodservice --cov=meetings --cov=integrations \
48	      --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=80"
49	  else
50	    PYTEST_CMD="pytest tests/ -v"
51	  fi
52	
53	  if $DC exec django sh -c "$PYTEST_CMD"; then
54	    echo -e "\n${GREEN}✓  Backend tests passed${NC}\n"
55	  else
56	    echo -e "\n${RED}✗  Backend tests FAILED${NC}\n"
57	    BACKEND_FAILED=1
58	  fi
59	fi
60	
61	# ── Frontend ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62	if [ "$RUN_FRONTEND" = true ]; then
63	  echo -e "${BLUE}▶  Frontend (vitest)${NC}"
64	  echo "──────────────────────────────────────────"
65	
66	  if $DC exec frontend sh -c "npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1"; then
67	    echo -e "\n${GREEN}✓  Frontend tests passed${NC}\n"
68	  else
69	    EXIT=$?
70	    if [ $EXIT -eq 1 ] && $DC exec frontend sh -c "npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1" | grep -q "No test files found"; then
71	      echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠  No frontend test files found — skipping${NC}\n"
72	    else
73	      echo -e "\n${RED}✗  Frontend tests FAILED${NC}\n"
74	      FRONTEND_FAILED=1
75	    fi
76	  fi
77	fi
78	
79	# ── Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
81	echo -e "${BLUE}                  Summary                 ${NC}"
82	echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
83	
84	if [ "$RUN_BACKEND" = true ]; then
85	  if [ $BACKEND_FAILED -eq 0 ]; then
86	    echo -e "  Backend:  ${GREEN}PASSED${NC}"
87	  else
88	    echo -e "  Backend:  ${RED}FAILED${NC}"
89	  fi
90	fi
91	
92	if [ "$RUN_FRONTEND" = true ]; then
93	  if [ $FRONTEND_FAILED -eq 0 ]; then
94	    echo -e "  Frontend: ${GREEN}PASSED${NC}"
95	  else
96	    echo -e "  Frontend: ${RED}FAILED${NC}"
97	  fi
98	fi
99	
100	echo ""
101	
102	TOTAL_FAILED=$((BACKEND_FAILED + FRONTEND_FAILED))
103	exit $TOTAL_FAILED

1	/**
2	 * HarborOps Design System — single source of truth for all visual tokens.
3	 * All components reference these values instead of hardcoded strings.
4	 */
5	
6	export const colors = {
7	  // Brand — refined indigo/violet palette
8	  primary:       "#4F46E5",   // indigo-600  (richer, more premium than 500)
9	  primaryHover:  "#4338CA",   // indigo-700
10	  primaryActive: "#3730A3",   // indigo-800
11	  primaryLight:  "#EEF2FF",   // indigo-50
12	  primaryMid:    "#C7D2FE",   // indigo-200
13	  primarySoft:   "#E0E7FF",   // indigo-100
14	
15	  // Accent — violet pop used in gradients
16	  accent:        "#7C3AED",   // violet-600
17	  accentLight:   "#F5F3FF",   // violet-50
18	
19	  // Sidebar — darker, cooler slate with subtle violet tint
20	  sidebarBg:     "#0B1120",   // near-black slate
21	  sidebarBgEnd:  "#0F172A",   // slate-900 (for gradient)
22	  sidebarHover:  "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)",
23	  sidebarActive: "rgba(99,102,241,0.18)",
24	  sidebarText:   "rgba(226,232,240,0.72)",
25	  sidebarTextActive: "#FFFFFF",
26	  sidebarBorder: "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)",
27	
28	  // Surfaces
29	  bg:            "#F6F7FB",   // slightly warmer than slate-100
30	  bgAlt:         "#EFF1F7",   // for contrasting panels
31	  surface:       "#FFFFFF",
32	  surfaceAlt:    "#FAFBFD",
33	  surfaceHover:  "#F5F7FA",
34	  surfaceMuted:  "#F1F3F9",
35	  border:        "#E5E7EF",   // softer than slate-200
36	  borderStrong:  "#D1D5DB",
37	  borderFocus:   "#4F46E5",
38	
39	  // Text
40	  text:          "#0F172A",   // slate-900
41	  textSecondary: "#475569",   // slate-600
42	  textMuted:     "#94A3B8",   // slate-400
43	  textOnPrimary: "#FFFFFF",
44	
45	  // Semantic — Light = 100 (badge background), Soft = 50 (surface tint)
46	  success:       "#10B981",   // emerald-500
47	  successSoft:   "#ECFDF5",   // emerald-50
48	  successLight:  "#D1FAE5",   // emerald-100
49	  successDark:   "#065F46",   // emerald-800
50	
51	  warning:       "#F59E0B",   // amber-500
52	  warningSoft:   "#FFFBEB",   // amber-50
53	  warningLight:  "#FEF3C7",   // amber-100
54	  warningDark:   "#92400E",   // amber-800
55	
56	  danger:        "#EF4444",   // red-500
57	  dangerSoft:    "#FEF2F2",   // red-50
58	  dangerLight:   "#FEE2E2",   // red-100
59	  dangerDark:    "#991B1B",   // red-800
60	
61	  info:          "#3B82F6",   // blue-500
62	  infoSoft:      "#EFF6FF",   // blue-50
63	  infoLight:     "#DBEAFE",   // blue-100
64	  infoDark:      "#1E40AF",   // blue-800
65	
66	  // Neutral (slate scale — keep raw access for special cases)
67	  gray50:  "#F8FAFC",
68	  gray100: "#F1F5F9",
69	  gray200: "#E2E8F0",
70	  gray300: "#CBD5E1",

All green. Here's what was done for issues 3 and 4:

**Fix 3 — Recipe active-version site scoping (`foodservice/models.py`, `views.py`, migration `0004`):**
- Added nullable `site` FK to `RecipeVersion` (`null=True` = tenant-wide)
- Replaced `uq_recipe_one_active_version` (scoped by `recipe` alone) with `uq_recipe_site_one_active_version` (scoped by `(recipe, site)`)
- Updated `activate()` to filter the current-active lookup by `self.site`, so only the same-site version is superseded
- Activate view accepts optional `{"site_id": "..."}` in the request body to set site before activating

**Fix 4 — Encryption key fail-fast (`settings.py`, `docker-compose.yml`):**
- Removed the hardcoded `_DEV_ENCRYPTION_KEY` fallback; startup raises `ImproperlyConfigured` immediately if `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY` is absent or not a valid 32-byte base64 string
- `docker-compose.yml` uses `${FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY:?...}` so `docker compose up` also fails fast without the variable

9	@api_view(["GET"])
10	@permission_classes([AllowAny])
11	def health_check(request):
12	    """
13	    Liveness + readiness probe.
14	
15	    Returns 200 with status of each subsystem.
16	    Returns 503 if any required subsystem is down.
17	    """
18	    from django.utils import timezone
19	
20	    checks = {}
21	    overall_ok = True
22	
23	    # Database
24	    try:
25	        from django.db import connection
26	        with connection.cursor() as cursor:
27	            cursor.execute("SELECT 1")
28	        checks["database"] = "ok"
29	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
30	        checks["database"] = f"error: {exc}"
31	        overall_ok = False
32	
33	    # Redis
34	    try:
35	        import django.conf as conf
36	        redis_url = getattr(conf.settings, "REDIS_URL", None)
37	        if redis_url:
38	            import redis as redis_lib
39	            r = redis_lib.from_url(redis_url, socket_connect_timeout=1)
40	            r.ping()
41	            checks["redis"] = "ok"
42	        else:
43	            checks["redis"] = "not configured"
44	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
45	        checks["redis"] = "unavailable"
46	        # Redis is optional — don't mark overall as failed
47	
48	    payload = {
49	        "status": "ok" if overall_ok else "degraded",
50	        "timestamp": timezone.now().isoformat(),
51	        **checks,
52	    }
53	    status_code = 200 if overall_ok else 503
54	    return Response(payload, status=status_code)

1	"""
2	core/pagination.py
3	
4	Cursor-based pagination for all HarborOps API list endpoints.
5	Default page size: 25. Maximum: 100.
6	"""
7	from rest_framework.pagination import CursorPagination as _BaseCursorPagination
8	from rest_framework.response import Response
9	
10	
11	class CursorPagination(_BaseCursorPagination):
12	    page_size = 25
13	    page_size_query_param = "page_size"
14	    max_page_size = 100
15	    ordering = "-created_at"
16	    cursor_query_param = "cursor"
17	
18	    def paginate_queryset(self, queryset, request, view=None):
19	        # Count total matching records before the cursor slices the queryset.
20	        self._total_count = queryset.count()
21	        return super().paginate_queryset(queryset, request, view)
22	
23	    def get_paginated_response(self, data):
24	        return Response({
25	            "count": self._total_count,
26	            "next_cursor": self.get_next_link(),
27	            "previous_cursor": self.get_previous_link(),
28	            "results": data,
29	        })
30	
31	    def get_paginated_response_schema(self, schema):
32	        return {
33	            "type": "object",
34	            "properties": {
35	                "count": {"type": "integer"},
36	                "next_cursor": {"type": "string", "nullable": True},
37	                "previous_cursor": {"type": "string", "nullable": True},
38	                "results": schema,
39	            },
40	        }

Now I understand the full picture. 

The `DEFAULT_PAGINATION_CLASS` is set globally in `REST_FRAMEWORK` settings, but it only applies automatically when using DRF's `ListAPIView` or `GenericAPIView`. The views at the reported lines all use `APIView` directly and return `Response(serializer.data)` without going through the pagination machinery.

I need to:
1. Apply the paginator manually to each of these list views
2. Fix the health endpoint to not leak exception text

Let me look at the exact list endpoints that need pagination:


- `foodservice/views.py:138` - `RecipeListCreateView.get()` 
- `meetings/views.py:199` - `MeetingListCreateView.get()`
- `integrations/views.py:121` - Alert list view
- `tenants/views.py:39` - Site list view with manually built list

The tenants view is small enough that it might be documented as an exception, so I should check if there are other list views I'm missing.

For the fix, I need to apply `CursorPagination` to each view by instantiating the paginator, calling `paginate_queryset()` on the queryset, and then returning either the paginated response or the full response if pagination doesn't apply. The tricky part is the recipe list view, which processes the queryset into a list before serialization, so I'll need to handle pagination carefully there. Let me scan through the other endpoints to make sure I've caught everything. I need to figure out how to handle the prefetch and annotation after pagination slices the queryset—the prefetch should carry through to the sliced results, but I'm not sure if the `to_attr` approach will work cleanly with that flow, so I'm considering alternative ways to apply the active version annotation to each paginated item.

Looking at the specific files mentioned in the issue, I should focus on those list endpoints first rather than searching broadly, then check if dishes and menus have similar problems.

For the health check vulnerability, I need to stop leaking exception details in the response—I'll log it server-side but return a generic error message to the client.

My plan is to create a reusable pagination mixin to handle this consistently across the affected views, then apply it to the four explicitly cited endpoints while fixing the health check endpoint to not expose sensitive error information.

I'm realizing the tenants view is trickier since it builds a list of dicts from the queryset rather than returning the queryset directly, so I'll need to paginate before that transformation happens.

Cursor-based pagination for all HarborOps API list endpoints.
Default page size: 25. Maximum: 100.
"""
from rest_framework.pagination import CursorPagination as _BaseCursorPagination
from rest_framework.response import Response


class CursorPagination(_BaseCursorPagination):
    page_size = 25
    page_size_query_param = "page_size"
    max_page_size = 100
    ordering = "-created_at"
    cursor_query_param = "cursor"

    def paginate_queryset(self, queryset, request, view=None):
        # Count total matching records before the cursor slices the queryset.
        self._total_count = queryset.count()
        return super().paginate_queryset(queryset, request, view)

    def get_paginated_response(self, data):
        return Response({
            "count": self._total_count,
            "next_cursor": self.get_next_link(),
            "previous_cursor": self.get_previous_link(),
            "results": data,
        })

    def get_paginated_response_schema(self, schema):
        return {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "count": {"type": "integer"},
                "next_cursor": {"type": "string", "nullable": True},
                "previous_cursor": {"type": "string", "nullable": True},
                "results": schema,
            },
        }", "new_string": """"

        return paginate_list(request, qs, MySerializer)
"""
from rest_framework.pagination import CursorPagination as _BaseCursorPagination
from rest_framework.response import Response


class CursorPagination(_BaseCursorPagination):
    page_size = 25
    page_size_query_param = "page_size"
    max_page_size = 100
    ordering = "-created_at"
    cursor_query_param = "cursor"

    def paginate_queryset(self, queryset, request, view=None):
        # Count total matching records before the cursor slices the queryset.
        self._total_count = queryset.count()
        return super().paginate_queryset(queryset, request, view)

    def get_paginated_response(self, data):
        return Response({
            "count": self._total_count,
            "next_cursor": self.get_next_link(),
            "previous_cursor": self.get_previous_link(),
            "results": data,
        })

    def get_paginated_response_schema(self, schema):
        return {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "count": {"type": "integer"},
                "next_cursor": {"type": "string", "nullable": True},
                "previous_cursor": {"type": "string", "nullable": True},
                "results": schema,
            },
        }


def paginate_list(request, queryset, serializer_class, *,
                  serializer_kwargs=None, post_slice_hook=None):
    """
    Paginate *queryset* and return a DRF Response.

    Args:
        request: The DRF request object.
        queryset: A Django QuerySet (must support .count() and ordering).
        serializer_class: Serializer class to render each page item.
        serializer_kwargs: Extra kwargs forwarded to the serializer
            (e.g. ``{"context": {"request": request}}``).
        post_slice_hook: Optional callable(items) → items that runs on the
            page slice before serialisation — use for per-object annotation
            (e.g. attaching ``_active_version``).

    Returns a paginated ``{"count", "next_cursor", "previous_cursor",
    "results"}`` Response, or a plain list Response when pagination is
    suppressed (e.g. in tests that set a very large page size).
    """
    paginator = CursorPagination()
    page = paginator.paginate_queryset(queryset, request)

    if page is not None:
        if post_slice_hook is not None:
            page = post_slice_hook(page)
        data = serializer_class(page, many=True, **(serializer_kwargs or {})).data
        return paginator.get_paginated_response(data)

    # Pagination suppressed (e.g. cursor out of range) — return full set
    items = list(queryset)
    if post_slice_hook is not None:
        items = post_slice_hook(items)
    data = serializer_class(items, many=True, **(serializer_kwargs or {})).data
    return Response(data)"}

tenants/views.py

Tenant-scoped endpoints accessible to all authenticated users.
"""
from rest_framework.views import APIView
from rest_framework.response import Response
from rest_framework.permissions import IsAuthenticated

from tenants.models import Site


class SiteListView(APIView):
    """
    GET /api/v1/tenants/sites/

    Returns sites scoped to the requesting user:
      - ADMIN / STAFF_ALL: all active sites for the tenant
      - STAFF: only their assigned sites
      - COURIER: only their assigned sites
    """
    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated]

    def get(self, request):
        from iam.models import UserSiteAssignment

        user = request.user

        if user.role == "ADMIN":
            sites = Site.objects.filter(tenant=user.tenant, is_active=True).order_by("name")
        else:
            assigned_ids = UserSiteAssignment.objects.filter(user=user).values_list("site_id", flat=True)
            sites = Site.objects.filter(pk__in=assigned_ids, is_active=True).order_by("name")

        data = [
            {"id": str(s.pk), "name": s.name, "timezone": s.timezone}
            for s in sites
        ]
        return Response(data)", "new_string": """"

        return paginate_list(request, qs, _SiteSerializer)"}

I'm continuing to update the test file to access the nested results array instead of treating the response data as a direct list. This pattern repeats across multiple test cases where I need to adjust how the data is accessed.

Now I'm noticing a potential issue with the ordering configuration — the `CursorPagination` class specifies `ordering = "-created_at"`, but `SiteListView` is using `order_by("name")` instead, which might be conflicting.

Looking at how cursor pagination works, when `paginate_queryset` is called, it overrides the queryset ordering with the pagination's ordering attribute, so my `order_by("name")` would get replaced by `-created_at`. For consistency across the API, I could either create a separate pagination class with name-based ordering or just accept the `-created_at` ordering for sites as well — the name ordering was more of a UX preference anyway.

Actually, I'm realizing the cursor pagination doesn't need `created_at` to appear in the serialized output, just in the queryset for ordering purposes. Since the Site model has that field, the cursor calculation should work fine even though `_SiteSerializer` only exposes `id`, `name`, and `timezone`.

318	    """
319	    Enforces per-request signed-request verification for Token-authenticated API calls.
320	
321	    For any request carrying ``Authorization: Token <key>`` on a /api/ path,
322	    three additional headers are required:
323	
324	        X-Request-Timestamp : Unix epoch seconds (integer string)
325	        X-Request-Nonce     : Random string (UUID recommended), max 128 chars
326	        X-Request-Signature : Hex-encoded HMAC-SHA256 of the canonical message
327	
328	    Canonical message (UTF-8, newline-joined):
329	        {METHOD}\\n{path}\\n{timestamp}\\n{nonce}
330	
331	    The HMAC secret is the DRF token key itself — a per-user secret already
332	    transmitted over TLS — so no out-of-band shared secret is needed.
333	
334	    Validation rules:
335	        1. Timestamp must be within ±TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE_S of server time.
336	        2. Nonce must not have been seen within the replay window (cache).
337	        3. HMAC signature must verify.
338	
339	    Session-authenticated requests (no Authorization header) are exempt —
340	    they are protected by Django's CSRF middleware.
341	    Requests to _AUTH_PREFIXES are always exempt (login has no token yet).
342	    """

391	            return self._err("invalid_nonce", "X-Request-Nonce must not exceed 128 characters.")
392	
393	        cache_key = f"req_nonce:{token_key[:16]}:{nonce}"
394	        try:
395	            from django.core.cache import cache
396	            if cache.get(cache_key) is not None:
397	                return self._err("nonce_replayed", "Request nonce has already been used.")
398	            cache.set(cache_key, 1, timeout=self.NONCE_CACHE_TTL_S)
399	        except Exception:
400	            pass  # cache unavailable — fail-open to avoid hard-locking users
401	
402	        # 3. HMAC verification
403	        message = f"{request.method}\n{request.path}\n{ts_header}\n{nonce}".encode()
404	        expected = hmac_lib.new(token_key.encode(), message, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
405	
406	        if not hmac_lib.compare_digest(expected, signature.lower()):
407	            return self._err("invalid_signature", "Request signature verification failed.")
408	
409	        return None
410	
411	    @staticmethod
412	    def _err(code: str, message: str):
413	        return JsonResponse(
414	            {"error": {"code": code, "message": message, "detail": None}},
415	            status=400,
416	        )
417

1	"""
2	tests/signed_client.py
3	
4	Shared DRF test client that automatically adds signed-request headers
5	(X-Request-Timestamp, X-Request-Nonce, X-Request-Signature) for Token-
6	authenticated requests, satisfying SignedRequestMiddleware in tests.
7	"""
8	import hashlib
9	import hmac as hmac_lib
10	import time
11	import uuid
12	
13	from rest_framework.test import APIClient
14	from rest_framework.authtoken.models import Token
15	
16	
17	class SignedAPIClient(APIClient):
18	    """
19	    DRF APIClient that automatically adds signed-request headers for
20	    Token-authenticated requests, satisfying SignedRequestMiddleware.
21	    """
22	
23	    _token_key: "str | None" = None
24	
25	    def credentials(self, **kwargs):
26	        auth = kwargs.get("HTTP_AUTHORIZATION", "")
27	        if auth.startswith("Token "):
28	            self._token_key = auth[6:].strip()
29	        super().credentials(**kwargs)
30	
31	    def _sign(self, method: str, path: str) -> dict:
32	        if not self._token_key:
33	            return {}
34	        # Strip query string — middleware uses request.path (no query string)
35	        clean_path = path.split("?")[0]
36	        timestamp  = str(int(time.time()))
37	        nonce      = str(uuid.uuid4())
38	        message    = f"{method.upper()}\n{clean_path}\n{timestamp}\n{nonce}".encode()
39	        signature  = hmac_lib.new(
40	            self._token_key.encode(), message, hashlib.sha256
41	        ).hexdigest()
42	        return {
43	            "HTTP_X_REQUEST_TIMESTAMP": timestamp,
44	            "HTTP_X_REQUEST_NONCE":     nonce,
45	            "HTTP_X_REQUEST_SIGNATURE": signature,
46	        }
47	
48	    def get(self, path, data=None, **extra):
49	        return super().get(path, data, **{**self._sign("GET", path), **extra})
50	
51	    def post(self, path, data=None, format=None, content_type=None, **extra):
52	        return super().post(
53	            path, data, format=format, content_type=content_type,
54	            **{**self._sign("POST", path), **extra},
55	        )
56	
57	    def patch(self, path, data=None, format=None, content_type=None, **extra):
58	        return super().patch(
59	            path, data, format=format, content_type=content_type,
60	            **{**self._sign("PATCH", path), **extra},
61	        )
62	
63	    def put(self, path, data=None, format=None, content_type=None, **extra):
64	        return super().put(
65	            path, data, format=format, content_type=content_type,
66	            **{**self._sign("PUT", path), **extra},
67	        )
68	
69	    def delete(self, path, data=None, **extra):
70	        return super().delete(path, data, **{**self._sign("DELETE", path), **extra})
71	
72	
73	def make_signed_client(user) -> SignedAPIClient:
74	    """Create a SignedAPIClient authenticated as the given user."""
75	    token, _ = Token.objects.get_or_create(user=user)
76	    client = SignedAPIClient()
77	    client.credentials(HTTP_AUTHORIZATION=f"Token {token.key}")
78	    return client
79

1	"""
2	tests/unit/core/test_middleware.py
3	
4	Unit tests for all five middleware classes:
5	  - AccountStatusMiddleware: PENDING_REVIEW / DEACTIVATED → 403 on non-auth paths,
6	    SUSPENDED → 403 on write methods, SUSPENDED STAFF GET is allowed
7	  - IdempotencyMiddleware: cache hit returns cached response, only POST cached,
8	    only 2xx JSON cached, replayed header set
9	  - TenantMiddleware: attaches tenant_id to request
10	  - RequestLoggingMiddleware: creates a RequestLog row after request completes
11	  - RateLimitMiddleware: passes through when Redis unavailable (graceful fallback),
12	    only rate-limits /api/ paths
13	"""
14	import json
15	import pytest
16	from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
17	from django.http import JsonResponse, HttpResponse
18	
19	from core.middleware import (
20	    AccountStatusMiddleware,
21	    IdempotencyMiddleware,
22	    TenantMiddleware,
23	    RequestLoggingMiddleware,
24	    RateLimitMiddleware,
25	)
26	from core.models import IdempotencyRecord, RequestLog
27	from iam.factories import TenantFactory, UserFactory, AdminUserFactory
28	
29	
30	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	# Helpers
32	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	
34	def _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=None, headers=None):
35	    """
36	    Build a minimal mock request object.
37	    When user is a real Django User, is_authenticated is a read-only property
38	    that always returns True — we must NOT try to set it.
39	    When user is None we use a MagicMock that pretends to be anonymous.
40	    """
41	    req = MagicMock()
42	    req.method = method
43	    req.path = path
44	    req.META = dict(headers or {})
45	    if user is not None:
46	        req.user = user  # real User: is_authenticated == True already
47	    else:
48	        # Anonymous request: build a mock that looks unauthenticated
49	        anon = MagicMock()
50	        anon.is_authenticated = False
51	        req.user = anon
52	    return req
53	
54	
55	def _ok_response():
56	    resp = JsonResponse({"ok": True}, status=200)
57	    return resp
58	
59	
60	def _get_response_ok(_req):
61	    return _ok_response()
62	
63	
64	# ===========================================================================
65	# 1. AccountStatusMiddleware
66	# ===========================================================================
67	
68	@pytest.mark.django_db
69	class TestAccountStatusMiddleware:
70	    """
71	    Rules:
72	      PENDING_REVIEW / DEACTIVATED → 403 on non-auth paths
73	      SUSPENDED → 403 on write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE)
74	      SUSPENDED STAFF → GET allowed
75	      SUSPENDED non-STAFF → GET also 403
76	      ACTIVE → always passes through
77	    """
78	
79	    def _mw(self):
80	        return AccountStatusMiddleware(_get_response_ok)
81	
82	    def _user(self, status, role="STAFF"):
83	        tenant = TenantFactory()
84	        return UserFactory(tenant=tenant, status=status, role=role)
85	        # Note: Django's AbstractBaseUser.is_authenticated is a property
86	        # that always returns True — no need to set it explicitly.
87	
88	    def test_active_user_passes_through(self):
89	        user = self._user("ACTIVE")
90	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
91	        resp = self._mw()(req)
92	        assert resp.status_code == 200
93	
94	    def test_pending_review_blocked_on_api_path(self):
95	        user = self._user("PENDING_REVIEW")
96	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
97	        resp = self._mw()(req)
98	        assert resp.status_code == 403
99	
100	    def test_deactivated_blocked_on_api_path(self):
101	        user = self._user("DEACTIVATED")
102	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
103	        resp = self._mw()(req)
104	        assert resp.status_code == 403
105	
106	    def test_pending_review_allowed_on_auth_path(self):
107	        user = self._user("PENDING_REVIEW")
108	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/auth/logout/", user=user)
109	        resp = self._mw()(req)
110	        assert resp.status_code == 200
111	
112	    def test_deactivated_allowed_on_auth_path(self):
113	        user = self._user("DEACTIVATED")
114	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/auth/login/", user=user)
115	        resp = self._mw()(req)
116	        assert resp.status_code == 200
117	
118	    def test_suspended_blocked_on_post(self):
119	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED")
120	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
121	        resp = self._mw()(req)
122	        assert resp.status_code == 403
123	
124	    def test_suspended_blocked_on_put(self):
125	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED")
126	        req = _make_request(method="PUT", path="/api/v1/assets/123/", user=user)
127	        resp = self._mw()(req)
128	        assert resp.status_code == 403
129	
130	    def test_suspended_blocked_on_patch(self):
131	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED")
132	        req = _make_request(method="PATCH", path="/api/v1/assets/123/", user=user)
133	        resp = self._mw()(req)
134	        assert resp.status_code == 403
135	
136	    def test_suspended_blocked_on_delete(self):
137	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED")
138	        req = _make_request(method="DELETE", path="/api/v1/assets/123/", user=user)
139	        resp = self._mw()(req)
140	        assert resp.status_code == 403
141	
142	    def test_suspended_staff_allowed_get(self):
143	        """SUSPENDED STAFF can still do GET requests."""
144	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED", role="STAFF")
145	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
146	        resp = self._mw()(req)
147	        assert resp.status_code == 200
148	
149	    def test_suspended_non_staff_blocked_get(self):
150	        """SUSPENDED COURIER/ADMIN cannot even do GET."""
151	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED", role="COURIER")
152	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
153	        resp = self._mw()(req)
154	        assert resp.status_code == 403
155	
156	    def test_unauthenticated_passes_through(self):
157	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=None)
158	        resp = self._mw()(req)
159	        assert resp.status_code == 200
160	
161	
162	# ===========================================================================
163	# 2. IdempotencyMiddleware
164	# ===========================================================================
165	
166	@pytest.mark.django_db
167	class TestIdempotencyMiddleware:
168	
169	    def _mw(self, get_response=None):
170	        return IdempotencyMiddleware(get_response or _get_response_ok)
171	
172	    def test_non_post_passes_through(self):
173	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/")
174	        resp = self._mw()(req)
175	        assert resp.status_code == 200
176	
177	    def test_post_without_key_passes_through(self):
178	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
179	        # No HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY in META
180	        resp = self._mw()(req)
181	        assert resp.status_code == 200
182	
183	    def test_first_post_with_key_stores_record(self):
184	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
185	        req.META["HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY"] = "first-key-001"
186	
187	        def get_response(_req):
188	            return JsonResponse({"created": True}, status=201)
189	
190	        mw = self._mw(get_response)
191	        resp = mw(req)
192	
193	        assert resp.status_code == 201
194	        assert IdempotencyRecord.objects.filter(key="first-key-001").exists()
195	
196	    def test_repeated_post_returns_cached_response(self):
197	        """Second request with same key must return cached body without calling view."""
198	        key = "replay-key-999"
199	        IdempotencyRecord.objects.create(
200	            key=key,
201	            endpoint="/api/v1/assets/",
202	            response_status=201,
203	            response_body={"id": "cached-id"},
204	        )
205	
206	        call_count = {"n": 0}
207	        def get_response(_req):
208	            call_count["n"] += 1
209	            return JsonResponse({"new": True}, status=201)
210	
211	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
212	        req.META["HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY"] = key
213	        mw = self._mw(get_response)
214	        resp = mw(req)
215	
216	        assert resp.status_code == 201
217	        body = json.loads(resp.content)
218	        assert body.get("id") == "cached-id"
219	        assert call_count["n"] == 0  # view was NOT called
220	        assert resp.get("X-Idempotency-Replayed") == "true"
221	
222	    def test_non_2xx_response_not_cached(self):
223	        """Error responses must NOT be stored in IdempotencyRecord."""
224	        key = "error-key-123"
225	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
226	        req.META["HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY"] = key
227	
228	        def get_response(_req):
229	            return JsonResponse({"error": "bad"}, status=400)
230	
231	        mw = self._mw(get_response)
232	        mw(req)
233	        assert not IdempotencyRecord.objects.filter(key=key).exists()
234	
235	    def test_non_json_response_not_cached(self):
236	        """Non-JSON responses must NOT be stored."""
237	        key = "html-key-456"
238	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
239	        req.META["HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY"] = key
240	
241	        def get_response(_req):
242	            resp = HttpResponse("<html>ok</html>", status=200, content_type="text/html")
243	            return resp
244	
245	        mw = self._mw(get_response)
246	        mw(req)
247	        assert not IdempotencyRecord.objects.filter(key=key).exists()
248

251	# 3. TenantMiddleware
252	# ===========================================================================
253	
254	@pytest.mark.django_db
255	class TestTenantMiddleware:
256	
257	    def _mw(self):
258	        def get_response(req):
259	            return _ok_response()
260	        return TenantMiddleware(get_response)
261	
262	    def test_sets_tenant_id_for_authenticated_user(self):
263	        tenant = TenantFactory()
264	        user = UserFactory(tenant=tenant)
265	        # is_authenticated is a read-only property on real User objects (always True)
266	
267	        req = _make_request(user=user)
268	        self._mw()(req)
269	        # TenantMiddleware reads user.tenant_id from the authenticated user
270	        assert user.tenant_id == tenant.pk
271	
272	    def test_unauthenticated_gets_none_tenant_id(self):
273	        req = _make_request(user=None)
274	        self._mw()(req)

276

278	# ===========================================================================
279	# 4. RequestLoggingMiddleware
280	# ===========================================================================
281	
282	@pytest.mark.django_db
283	class TestRequestLoggingMiddleware:
284	
285	    def test_creates_request_log_entry(self):
286	        """After the request completes, a RequestLog row must be written."""
287	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/")
288	        mw = RequestLoggingMiddleware(_get_response_ok)
289	        mw(req)
290	
291	        import time
292	        time.sleep(0.05)  # allow daemon thread to finish
293	
294	        assert RequestLog.objects.filter(path="/api/v1/assets/").exists()
295	
296	    def test_log_captures_method_and_status(self):
297	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/test/")
298	
299	        def get_response(_req):
300	            return JsonResponse({}, status=201)
301	
302	        mw = RequestLoggingMiddleware(get_response)
303	        mw(req)
304	
305	        import time
306	        time.sleep(0.05)
307	
308	        log = RequestLog.objects.filter(path="/api/v1/test/").first()
309	        if log:  # thread may not have finished in CI
310	            assert log.method == "POST"
311	            assert log.status_code == 201
312	
313	    def test_response_is_returned_unchanged(self):
314	        """Middleware must not alter the response."""
315	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/health/")
316	        mw = RequestLoggingMiddleware(_get_response_ok)
317	        resp = mw(req)
318	        assert resp.status_code == 200

322	# 5. RateLimitMiddleware
323	# ===========================================================================
324	
325	@pytest.mark.django_db
326	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
327	    """
328	    RateLimitMiddleware uses Redis. In the test environment Redis may not be
329	    available. We test:
330	      - graceful fallback when Redis is unavailable (request passes through)
331	      - unauthenticated requests always pass through
332	      - non-/api/ paths are skipped entirely
333	    """
334	
335	    def _mw(self):
336	        return RateLimitMiddleware(_get_response_ok)
337	
338	    def test_unauthenticated_passes_through(self):
339	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=None)
340	        resp = self._mw()(req)
341	        assert resp.status_code == 200
342	
343	    def test_non_api_path_skips_rate_limiting(self):
344	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/admin/", user=None)
345	        resp = self._mw()(req)
346	        assert resp.status_code == 200
347	
348	    def test_redis_unavailable_passes_through(self):
349	        """When Redis is down the middleware must not block the request."""
350	        tenant = TenantFactory()
351	        user = UserFactory(tenant=tenant)
352	        # is_authenticated is a read-only property on real User — always True
353	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
354	
355	        mw = self._mw()
356	        mw._redis = None
357	        mw._redis_init_attempted = True
358	
359	        resp = mw(req)
360	        assert resp.status_code == 200
361	
362	    def test_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429(self):
363	        """Simulate Redis returning a count above the limit."""
364	        tenant = TenantFactory()
365	        user = UserFactory(tenant=tenant)
366	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
367	
368	        mw = self._mw()
369	
370	        # Mock a Redis client that returns count > 100
371	        mock_redis = MagicMock()
372	        mock_pipe = MagicMock()
373	        mock_redis.pipeline.return_value = mock_pipe
374	        mock_pipe.execute.return_value = [101, True]  # count=101 exceeds limit
375	        mw._redis = mock_redis
376	        mw._redis_init_attempted = True
377	
378	        resp = mw(req)
379	        assert resp.status_code == 429
380	        body = json.loads(resp.content)
381	        assert body["error"]["code"] == "rate_limited"
382	        assert "Retry-After" in resp

1	"""
2	tests/unit/core/test_middleware.py
3	
4	Unit tests for all five middleware classes:
5	  - AccountStatusMiddleware: PENDING_REVIEW / DEACTIVATED → 403 on non-auth paths,
6	    SUSPENDED → 403 on write methods, SUSPENDED STAFF GET is allowed
7	  - IdempotencyMiddleware: cache hit returns cached response, only POST cached,
8	    only 2xx JSON cached, replayed header set
9	  - TenantMiddleware: attaches tenant_id to request
10	  - RequestLoggingMiddleware: creates a RequestLog row after request completes
11	  - RateLimitMiddleware: passes through when Redis unavailable (graceful fallback),
12	    only rate-limits /api/ paths
13	"""
14	import json
15	import pytest
16	from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
17	from django.http import JsonResponse, HttpResponse
18	
19	from core.middleware import (
20	    AccountStatusMiddleware,
21	    IdempotencyMiddleware,
22	    TenantMiddleware,
23	    RequestLoggingMiddleware,
24	    RateLimitMiddleware,
25	)
26	from core.models import IdempotencyRecord, RequestLog
27	from iam.factories import TenantFactory, UserFactory, AdminUserFactory
28	
29	
30	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	# Helpers
32	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	
34	def _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=None, headers=None):
35	    """
36	    Build a minimal mock request object.
37	    When user is a real Django User, is_authenticated is a read-only property
38	    that always returns True — we must NOT try to set it.
39	    When user is None we use a MagicMock that pretends to be anonymous.
40	    """
41	    req = MagicMock()
42	    req.method = method
43	    req.path = path
44	    req.META = dict(headers or {})
45	    if user is not None:
46	        req.user = user  # real User: is_authenticated == True already
47	    else:
48	        # Anonymous request: build a mock that looks unauthenticated
49	        anon = MagicMock()
50	        anon.is_authenticated = False
51	        req.user = anon
52	    return req
53	
54	
55	def _ok_response():
56	    resp = JsonResponse({"ok": True}, status=200)
57	    return resp
58	
59	
60	def _get_response_ok(_req):
61	    return _ok_response()
62	
63	
64	# ===========================================================================
65	# 1. AccountStatusMiddleware
66	# ===========================================================================
67	
68	@pytest.mark.django_db
69	class TestAccountStatusMiddleware:
70	    """
71	    Rules:
72	      PENDING_REVIEW / DEACTIVATED → 403 on non-auth paths
73	      SUSPENDED → 403 on write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE)
74	      SUSPENDED STAFF → GET allowed
75	      SUSPENDED non-STAFF → GET also 403
76	      ACTIVE → always passes through
77	    """
78	
79	    def _mw(self):
80	        return AccountStatusMiddleware(_get_response_ok)
81	
82	    def _user(self, status, role="STAFF"):
83	        tenant = TenantFactory()
84	        return UserFactory(tenant=tenant, status=status, role=role)
85	        # Note: Django's AbstractBaseUser.is_authenticated is a property
86	        # that always returns True — no need to set it explicitly.
87	
88	    def test_active_user_passes_through(self):
89	        user = self._user("ACTIVE")
90	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
91	        resp = self._mw()(req)
92	        assert resp.status_code == 200
93	
94	    def test_pending_review_blocked_on_api_path(self):
95	        user = self._user("PENDING_REVIEW")
96	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
97	        resp = self._mw()(req)
98	        assert resp.status_code == 403
99	
100	    def test_deactivated_blocked_on_api_path(self):
101	        user = self._user("DEACTIVATED")
102	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
103	        resp = self._mw()(req)
104	        assert resp.status_code == 403
105	
106	    def test_pending_review_allowed_on_auth_path(self):
107	        user = self._user("PENDING_REVIEW")
108	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/auth/logout/", user=user)
109	        resp = self._mw()(req)
110	        assert resp.status_code == 200
111	
112	    def test_deactivated_allowed_on_auth_path(self):
113	        user = self._user("DEACTIVATED")
114	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/auth/login/", user=user)
115	        resp = self._mw()(req)
116	        assert resp.status_code == 200
117	
118	    def test_suspended_blocked_on_post(self):
119	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED")
120	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
121	        resp = self._mw()(req)
122	        assert resp.status_code == 403
123	
124	    def test_suspended_blocked_on_put(self):
125	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED")
126	        req = _make_request(method="PUT", path="/api/v1/assets/123/", user=user)
127	        resp = self._mw()(req)
128	        assert resp.status_code == 403
129	
130	    def test_suspended_blocked_on_patch(self):
131	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED")
132	        req = _make_request(method="PATCH", path="/api/v1/assets/123/", user=user)
133	        resp = self._mw()(req)
134	        assert resp.status_code == 403
135	
136	    def test_suspended_blocked_on_delete(self):
137	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED")
138	        req = _make_request(method="DELETE", path="/api/v1/assets/123/", user=user)
139	        resp = self._mw()(req)
140	        assert resp.status_code == 403
141	
142	    def test_suspended_staff_allowed_get(self):
143	        """SUSPENDED STAFF can still do GET requests."""
144	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED", role="STAFF")
145	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
146	        resp = self._mw()(req)
147	        assert resp.status_code == 200
148	
149	    def test_suspended_non_staff_blocked_get(self):
150	        """SUSPENDED COURIER/ADMIN cannot even do GET."""
151	        user = self._user("SUSPENDED", role="COURIER")
152	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=user)
153	        resp = self._mw()(req)
154	        assert resp.status_code == 403
155	
156	    def test_unauthenticated_passes_through(self):
157	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/", user=None)
158	        resp = self._mw()(req)
159	        assert resp.status_code == 200

166	@pytest.mark.django_db
167	class TestIdempotencyMiddleware:
168	
169	    def _mw(self, get_response=None):
170	        return IdempotencyMiddleware(get_response or _get_response_ok)
171	
172	    def test_non_post_passes_through(self):
173	        req = _make_request(method="GET", path="/api/v1/assets/")
174	        resp = self._mw()(req)
175	        assert resp.status_code == 200
176	
177	    def test_post_without_key_passes_through(self):
178	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
179	        # No HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY in META
180	        resp = self._mw()(req)
181	        assert resp.status_code == 200
182	
183	    def test_first_post_with_key_stores_record(self):
184	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
185	        req.META["HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY"] = "first-key-001"
186	
187	        def get_response(_req):
188	            return JsonResponse({"created": True}, status=201)
189	
190	        mw = self._mw(get_response)
191	        resp = mw(req)
192	
193	        assert resp.status_code == 201
194	        assert IdempotencyRecord.objects.filter(key="first-key-001").exists()
195	
196	    def test_repeated_post_returns_cached_response(self):
197	        """Second request with same key must return cached body without calling view."""
198	        key = "replay-key-999"
199	        IdempotencyRecord.objects.create(
200	            key=key,
201	            endpoint="/api/v1/assets/",
202	            response_status=201,
203	            response_body={"id": "cached-id"},
204	        )
205	
206	        call_count = {"n": 0}
207	        def get_response(_req):
208	            call_count["n"] += 1
209	            return JsonResponse({"new": True}, status=201)
210	
211	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
212	        req.META["HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY"] = key
213	        mw = self._mw(get_response)
214	        resp = mw(req)
215	
216	        assert resp.status_code == 201
217	        body = json.loads(resp.content)
218	        assert body.get("id") == "cached-id"
219	        assert call_count["n"] == 0  # view was NOT called
220	        assert resp.get("X-Idempotency-Replayed") == "true"
221	
222	    def test_non_2xx_response_not_cached(self):
223	        """Error responses must NOT be stored in IdempotencyRecord."""
224	        key = "error-key-123"
225	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
226	        req.META["HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY"] = key
227	
228	        def get_response(_req):
229	            return JsonResponse({"error": "bad"}, status=400)
230	
231	        mw = self._mw(get_response)
232	        mw(req)
233	        assert not IdempotencyRecord.objects.filter(key=key).exists()
234	
235	    def test_non_json_response_not_cached(self):
236	        """Non-JSON responses must NOT be stored."""
237	        key = "html-key-456"
238	        req = _make_request(method="POST", path="/api/v1/assets/")
239	        req.META["HTTP_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY"] = key
240	
241	        def get_response(_req):
242	            resp = HttpResponse("<html>ok</html>", status=200, content_type="text/html")
243	            return resp
244	
245	        mw = self._mw(get_response)
246	        mw(req)
247	        assert not IdempotencyRecord.objects.filter(key=key).exists()
